title: Giving a Presentation to the Team

all.en: I had spent the whole weekend polishing my slides until they looked just right.
all.zh: 我整個週末都在修飾我的投影片，直到看起來剛剛好。

all.en: When Monday morning came, my stomach was twisting with nerves.
all.zh: 星期一早上一到，我的胃就因為緊張而打結。

all.en: The conference room slowly filled with colleagues holding mugs of coffee.
all.zh: 會議室慢慢坐滿了端著咖啡杯的同事。

all.en: I clicked on the first slide and reminded myself to breathe slowly.
all.zh: 我點開第一張投影片，提醒自己要慢慢呼吸。

all.en: My voice shook slightly at first, but it steadied once I got into the topic.
all.zh: 我的聲音一開始有點顫抖，但進入主題後就穩定了下來。

all.en: I shared the data clearly and used a short story to illustrate the main point.
all.zh: 我清楚地呈現數據，並用一個小故事來說明主要論點。

all.en: The senior manager nodded and even jotted something down in his notebook.
all.zh: 資深主管點頭表示認同，甚至在筆記本上記下幾筆。

all.en: One coworker raised a tough question about the projected costs.
all.zh: 一位同事提出一個關於預估成本的棘手問題。

all.en: I admitted I did not have the exact number but promised to follow up.
all.zh: 我坦承自己沒有確切數字，但承諾會後續追蹤回覆。

all.en: When I finished, the room gave a warm round of applause.
all.zh: 當我講完時，會議室裡響起溫暖的掌聲。

all.en: My boss patted my shoulder and said it was my best presentation yet.
all.zh: 我老闆拍了拍我的肩膀，說這是我目前最棒的一次簡報。

all.en: After the meeting ended, several colleagues stopped by my desk with thoughtful follow-up questions.
all.zh: 會議結束後，幾位同事繞到我座位旁，提出了一些深思熟慮的後續問題。

all.en: I jotted their feedback in a notebook so I could improve my next presentation.
all.zh: 我把他們的回饋寫進筆記本，以便改進下一次的簡報。

all.en: At lunch, my team treated me to bubble tea to celebrate the successful delivery.
all.zh: 午餐時，我的團隊請我喝珍珠奶茶，慶祝這次簡報順利完成。

all.en: In the afternoon, I researched the missing cost figures and built a clearer chart.
all.zh: 下午，我研究了缺漏的成本數據，並做了一張更清楚的圖表。

all.en: I emailed the updated information to everyone who had attended the morning session.
all.zh: 我把更新後的資訊以電子郵件寄給所有參加早上會議的同仁。

all.en: The senior manager replied quickly, calling my follow-up both prompt and professional.
all.zh: 資深主管很快回信，稱讚我的後續追蹤既迅速又專業。

all.en: He invited me to present the same topic to the executive committee next month.
all.zh: 他邀請我下個月向高階主管委員會做同樣的簡報。

all.en: My heart sank for a moment, but then a quiet confidence grew inside of me.
all.zh: 我的心一沉，但隨後一股安靜的自信在心中悄悄升起。

all.en: I started preparing earlier this time, rehearsing in front of a mirror every evening.
all.zh: 我這次更早開始準備，每天晚上都對著鏡子練習。

all.en: When the big day came, my nerves were still there, but they no longer controlled me.
all.zh: 當大日子來臨時，緊張感仍然存在，但它已不再支配我。

all.en: The executive committee listened intently as I clicked through every careful slide.
all.zh: 高階主管委員會專注聆聽，看著我點開每一張精心準備的投影片。

all.en: This time, I had memorized the cost figures and answered every question smoothly.
all.zh: 這一次，我已熟記成本數據，順暢地回答了每一個問題。

all.en: The CEO leaned forward and asked how I would scale the plan across regions.
all.zh: 執行長身體前傾，問我會如何把這個計畫擴展到各區域。

all.en: I outlined a phased approach, beginning with two pilot markets in the south.
all.zh: 我說明了一個分階段的做法，先從南部兩個試點市場開始。

all.en: He nodded approvingly and approved a six-month budget right there in the room.
all.zh: 他點頭表示肯定，當場就批准了六個月的預算。

all.en: After the meeting, my hands were shaking again, but only from sheer excitement.
all.zh: 會議結束後，我的雙手又開始顫抖，但這次純粹是因為興奮。

all.en: My boss congratulated me and said I had become the team's strongest presenter.
all.zh: 老闆向我道賀，說我已成為團隊裡最強的簡報者。

all.en: That weekend, I treated my parents to dinner at the restaurant they loved most.
all.zh: 那個週末，我請父母到他們最喜愛的餐廳吃晚餐。

all.en: I told them everything they had taught me about perseverance had finally paid off.
all.zh: 我告訴他們，他們教過我關於堅持的一切，終於開花結果了。

all.en: Looking ahead, I knew I had finally found my voice in a room full of leaders.
all.zh: 展望未來，我知道自己終於在滿是領導者的會議室中找到了屬於自己的聲音。

all.en: A few months later, my boss recommended me for a regional leadership training program.
all.zh: 幾個月後，老闆推薦我參加一個區域性的領導力培訓計畫。

all.en: I flew to the headquarters on a Sunday and met participants from twelve different countries.
all.zh: 我星期天飛到總部，認識了來自十二個不同國家的學員。

all.en: During the first session, each of us had to give a five-minute introduction speech.
all.zh: 在第一堂課上，每個人都要進行五分鐘的自我介紹演說。

all.en: I shared the story of my very first shaky presentation in front of the team.
all.zh: 我分享了自己在團隊面前第一次顫抖簡報的故事。

all.en: People laughed, then nodded, recognizing the same fear in their own early careers.
all.zh: 大家先是笑了，然後紛紛點頭，認出那是自己職涯早期同樣的恐懼。

all.en: Throughout the week, I practiced new techniques, including storytelling and silent pauses.
all.zh: 整整一週，我練習了新的技巧，包括說故事與適時的沉默停頓。

all.en: On the final day, I delivered a closing speech to all ninety international participants.
all.zh: 在最後一天，我向全部九十位國際學員發表了結業演說。

all.en: My hands stayed steady, and my voice carried clearly through the bright conference hall.
all.zh: 我的雙手保持平穩，聲音清晰地穿透了明亮的會議大廳。

all.en: After the applause faded, three regional directors invited me to lead future workshops.
all.zh: 掌聲落下後，三位區域總監邀請我未來協助帶領工作坊。

all.en: On the flight home, I quietly thought about how far one Monday morning could carry me.
all.zh: 在回程的飛機上，我靜靜思索著一個星期一的早晨能把我帶到多遠的地方。
